outlook.headline: New product wins offset by uneven power IC demand
outlook.summary: The next 1-2 quarters look mixed: Cirrus is broadening its analog power management portfolio with a new smart power IC that opens an attractive new application space, while Alpha & Omega is still seeing sharp power IC revenue declines both year over year and quarter over quarter. The slight uptick in mention activity suggests the topic is getting a bit more attention, but the reads do not yet show a broad-based inflection; watch whether Cirrus’ new product ramps fast enough to outweigh continued weakness elsewhere.

drivers.label: New smart power IC expands application space
drivers.detail: Cirrus says the new smart power IC opens an attractive new application space and broadens its product portfolio.

drivers.label: Sharp power IC revenue decline
drivers.detail: Alpha & Omega reported power IC revenue down sharply year over year and quarter over quarter, signaling ongoing demand weakness.

drivers.label: Slightly firmer mention activity
drivers.detail: Topic mentions are running modestly above the trailing average, implying a small uptick in attention into 2026Q1.

risks: Cirrus’ new smart power IC could stay niche or ramp slower than expected.
Alpha & Omega’s revenue weakness could persist into the next quarter, confirming broader softness.
The recent mention uptick could fade if there are no follow-on design wins or product announcements.
